behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI aggregation. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local parsing; command flags/output from git determine whether rename is surfaced as R vs add+delete. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: requires real filesystem + real git subprocess behavior to catch missing rename detection in actual invocation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is a deterministic git-summary CLI with no AI model or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, rename it, then run the CLI as a subprocess against that repo. Assert report shows the file only under `renamed` and not under `added`/`deleted`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Self-check passes: this test genuinely needs subprocess+real git rename semantics; a lower-level fake could miss invocation/flag issues.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is interaction with real git diff output for binary content and process I/O decoding. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: binary handling bugs often appear only with real repo files and subprocess stdout/stderr encoding/format. No AI dependency or multi-agent chain. Rejected higher levels: behavior is about git/binary diff handling only.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit a binary file change and run the CLI subprocess. Assert exit code is success, no crash, and binary file contributes to category counts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Self-check passes: real binary diff and subprocess output are required to catch real crashes.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is unborn-HEAD behavior from real git commands (often non-zero exit/message) and CLI error handling. REAL-INFRA: requires real git repo state with zero commits and real subprocess return handling. No LLM dependence or multi-step agentic flow. Rejected higher levels: behavior text specifies deterministic CLI/git state only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert it returns success with an empty structured report (not an exception/error output).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Self-check passes: exact failure appears at real git subprocess boundary, not just internal logic.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, which depends on real commit graph/index/worktree states plus CLI-git interaction. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git output can validate arithmetic but can miss real history query mistakes. No AI/agentic dependency. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic history accounting, not model judgment/pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Programmatically create a repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run CLI subprocess, and compare reported counts to expected counts derived from git history ground truth.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Self-check passes: validating against real history requires system-level execution.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is git index vs working-tree semantics and how CLI invokes git for each mode. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: only real git staging mechanics reliably expose mismatches. No AI model output and no multi-agent sequence. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is deterministic CLI comparison across git states.
behaviors.test_description: Create equivalent changes in two scenarios (staged vs unstaged) in a temp repo, run CLI subprocess for each, and assert structured stdout is byte-for-byte identical (or semantically identical if ordering is defined).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Self-check passes: requires real git index/worktree behavior; lower-level mocks can hide state-handling bugs.
